summary: Poisoned axios clone exfiltrates document.cookie to webhook.site
details: Package impersonates axios v0.18.0 (name 'v018-axios-cdntest', description 'Axios library v0.18.0') and ships a modified copy of axios with an appended browser-side payload. index.js defines window._axiosExfil, which reads document.cookie and issues an XMLHttpRequest GET to https://webhook.site/ef6e7978-f936-4664-b3ff-296a250e1735?c=<encoded-cookie>. A self-incriminating source comment ('// Poisoned axios@0.18.0 - cookie exfiltration payload') confirms intent. Any web application bundling this package would silently leak end-user session cookies to an attacker-controlled webhook endpoint, enabling session hijacking. The package has no author metadata, no repository, and is a single-file drop-in masquerading as the legitimate axios HTTP client.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
